name: Alto de La Bobia and Alto de El Segredal Loop from Luarca
name_original: Alto de La Bobia y Alto de El Segredal – circular desde Luarca
description: This difficult 40.1-mile road cycling route from Luarca tackles the challenging ascents of Alto de La Bobia and Alto de El Segredal, offerin
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Valdés are characterized by varied terrain, including coastal paths and inland roads. The region features a dynamic landscape with elevations that challenge cyclists, offering both moderate and difficult routes. These paths often traverse areas with significant elevation changes, providing diverse cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Valdés The most popular road cycling route is Alto de La Bobia and Alto de El Segredal Loop from Luarca, a 40.1 miles (64.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 35 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the Valdés landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Concha de Artedo Viaduct – Arroyo Ribón canyon loop from Caneiru, a difficult 51.2 miles (82.3 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs and scenic views of the canyon. Local road cyclists also love the Río Negro in Luarca – Luarca Lighthouse loop from Luarca, a 45.4 miles (73.0 km) trail leading through coastal areas and river valleys, often completed in about 3 hours 42 minutes. Road cycling around Valdés is defined by coastal roads, river valleys, and significant climbs. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Valdés are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 19 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Valdés's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Valdés?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes around Valdés, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes in Valdés?
A: Road cycling routes in Valdés are generally characterized as moderate to difficult. Out of the 43 available routes, 16 are considered moderate and 27 are difficult, featuring significant elevation gains and challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Valdés?
A: Yes, many routes in Valdés are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1078472793/alto-de-la-bobia-and-alto-de-el-segredal-loop-from-luarca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Bobia and Alto de El Segredal Loop from Luarca&lt;/a&gt;, or the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14988941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Negro in Luarca – Luarca Lighthouse loop from Luarca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Valdés?
A: The road cycling routes around Valdés feature varied terrain, including coastal paths, river valleys, and inland roads. You can expect dynamic landscapes with significant elevation changes, offering both challenging climbs and scenic descents.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see along the routes?
A: Many routes pass by interesting landmarks and natural features. You might cycle through the charming town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luarca&lt;/a&gt;, pass by the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3710397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luarca Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, or even encounter the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/865581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nonaya Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/363182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Regalina Chapel&lt;/a&gt; and the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/93692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach of Silence&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that offer particularly scenic viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32010115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Concha de Artedo Viaduct – Arroyo Ribón canyon loop from Caneiru&lt;/a&gt; offer challenging climbs rewarded with panoramic views of the canyon. Coastal routes also provide breathtaking vistas of the sea and dramatic cliffs.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Valdés?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 19 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging climbs, and the stunning coastal and river valley scenery that makes each ride unique.

Q: Can you recommend a challenging road cycling route in Valdés?
A: For a challenging experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14986318" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luarca Lighthouse – Esva River loop from Ḷḷuarca&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 76.8 km route features over 1100 meters of elevation gain and offers a rewarding ride through varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any historical sites accessible by road bike in Valdés?
A: Yes, you can cycle past several historical sites. For instance, the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Selgas Palace and the Jesús Nazareno Church‑Pantheon&lt;/a&gt; are notable historical attractions. The charming fishing village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luarca&lt;/a&gt; itself also boasts historical architecture.

Q: What is the longest road cycling route available in Valdés?
A: One of the longer routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32010115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Concha de Artedo Viaduct – Arroyo Ribón canyon loop from Caneiru&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 82.3 km and involves significant elevation, making it a demanding yet rewarding ride.

Q: Are there any routes that explore river valleys?
A: Yes, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14988941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Negro in Luarca – Luarca Lighthouse loop from Luarca&lt;/a&gt; lead through picturesque river valleys, offering a different scenic experience compared to the coastal paths.
tips: Ascent with a quiet road and beautiful views of the coast. Right at the end you can link up with the climb to the top of the segredal to continue the ascent. In the last curve before crowning there is a dirt track that after a few meters leads us to a panoramic view of western Asturias.

Paved and very quiet road that combines hard sections with breaks. Combined with La Bobia, you can climb 700m in altitude in a few kilometers without going too far from the coast. Good views of the coastline.
Nice picture of the Negro river in the beautiful town of Luarca.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult and is not suitable for beginners. It features significant elevation gain and demanding inclines, including sections with gradients above 15% on the Ascent to Alto de La Bobia and consistent 8% gradients on the Ascent to Alto de El Segredal. It requires very good physical condition and experience with challenging road cycling.

faqs.question: What is the terrain and road condition like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features a challenging mix of terrain. You'll encounter a succession of ramps and flats initially, leading into demanding climbs. The Alto de El Segredal has a series of ascents and descents with consistent 8% gradients over 5 kilometers. The Alto de La Bobia is even more challenging, with sustained gradients of 6-7% and ramps exceeding 15%, including sections of 2 kilometers at 13.5%. Road conditions can vary, with some segments potentially being unpaved or difficult for typical road bikes.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and views along the route?
faqs.answer: As you gain altitude, you'll be treated to beautiful coastal views and panoramic vistas of Western Asturias. The route passes through green meadows, dense Atlantic forests, and areas with views of reservoirs. The Sierra de La Bobia acts as a "natural balcony," offering impressive views of the valleys. Don't miss the challenging climbs of the Ascent to Alto de La Bobia and the Ascent to Alto de El Segredal, and the scenic Río Negro in Luarca at the start/end.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and is there parking available in Luarca?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route starting and ending in Luarca. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Luarca is a town with various public parking options. It's advisable to check local parking regulations and availability upon arrival, especially during peak seasons.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's exposure at higher elevations, especially to wind, and the demanding climbs, the best time to cycle would generally be during the milder months of late spring, summer, and early autumn. This avoids the harsher conditions of winter and ensures more pleasant temperatures for such a strenuous ride. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road cycling route in Asturias.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging road cycling route. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, this specific route is not recommended for cycling with dogs due to its length, difficulty, significant elevation changes, and the nature of road cycling on public roads.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: For this difficult route, you should bring a well-maintained road bike, appropriate cycling gear (helmet, padded shorts, gloves), plenty of water and nutrition, a repair kit (spare tubes, pump, multi-tool), a fully charged phone, and navigation (like the komoot app with the route downloaded). Due to potential exposure, consider layers of clothing, especially for wind protection at higher altitudes.

faqs.question: Are there any specific strategies for tackling the climbs, especially Alto de La Bobia?
faqs.answer: The Alto de La Bobia is known for its "labyrinth of roads" and numerous challenging ascents, some considered "Special Category." Be prepared for sustained steep gradients and ramps above 15%. Pacing yourself is crucial, and ensure you have appropriate gearing for such demanding climbs. Some sections might even require pushing your bike. You also have the option to chain the climb to Alto de El Segredal right after La Bobia if you're looking for an even greater challenge.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the Mtb Northern Route (Asturia) and the Northern Route (Asturia).

faqs.question: Are there places to refuel or rest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Luarca, where you'll find various cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. However, given the challenging and mountainous nature of the middle sections, amenities along the actual climbs of Alto de La Bobia and Alto de El Segredal may be limited. It's best to carry sufficient food and water for the duration of the ride.
